Audio:
Speaker 1: Morning, um, I'm making some breakfast for everyone, sweet potato pancakes and scrambled eggs. You know, I was thinking we could have a bit of a schedule for the week, okay so we can plan the lunches and after-school activities.

Speaker 2: That's a great idea, honey. Okay so, let's see, the kids have got sports practice and, um, the older son needs to finish his math homework. Can I, like, check the fridge for some fruit to add to their lunches?

Speaker 3: Actually, Mom, I already packed my lunch. It's got a sandwich, carrot sticks, and, um, a cookie.

Speaker 4: Mommy look! I made a picture with my crayons. See, it's a cat!

Speaker 5: Me too! Me too!

Speaker 1: Ah, nice job, honey. Um, so for lunch, I was thinking we could have some leftovers from last night, maybe, you know, some chicken and rice.

Speaker 2: That sounds good to me. Just, like, make sure the chicken is cooked through, okay?

Speaker 3: But why do we have to have leftovers? Can't we just have, like, pizza or something?

Speaker 4: I want pizza! I love pizza!

Speaker 5: No, no! Me too, pizza!

Speaker 1: Well, we can have pizza for dinner, but, um, leftovers are a good way to reduce food waste, you know?

Speaker 2: That's true, sweetie. Okay so, let's get the kids' lunches ready and then we can start on breakfast.

Speaker 3: Actually, can I go get my book? I need to finish reading it before school.

Speaker 4: Mommy, can I play with my dolls? I want to have a tea party.

Speaker 5: Look at this! I found a penny on the floor!

Speaker 2: Great job finding that, honey. Okay so, let's get the lunches ready and then we can, um, sit down to breakfast together.

Speaker 1: Sounds good to me. This weather is, um, really gloomy today, isn't it?

Speaker 2: Yeah, let's just stay inside and, like, enjoy a quiet morning at home.

Speaker 3: But why is it cold outside? Is it, like, because of the snow or something?

Speaker 4: I don't like the cold, Mommy. Can we have a blanket?

Speaker 5: No, I like the cold! I can go outside and play!

Speaker 1: Well, let's just stay inside for now, okay? I'll get the pancakes ready.

Speaker 2: Great, thanks, honey. Okay so, how's the day shaping up? Any, um, appointments or meetings?

Speaker 1: Um, just a meeting with the marketing team at, you know, 2 pm. That's about it.

Speaker 2: Okay, honey, let me see it. (pause) 

Speaker 3: Actually, I know where the picture is, it's on the fridge. 

Speaker 4: Mommy, can we have hot chocolate after we find the boots? 

Speaker 2: We can, but first let's find those boots. 

Speaker 5: No! I wanna see the picture! Look, look! 

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, sweetie, let's see the picture first. (laughs) 

Speaker 1: So, yeah, let's clean up this coat rack. Where are the boots? 

Speaker 4: I want to help! Can I help? 

Speaker 5: Me too! Me too! 

Speaker 1: Okay, great, you can help. Let's look together. 

Speaker 3: Can I ask, Dad? What's that thing on your shirt? Is it a coffee stain? 

Speaker 1: Ah, yeah, it's a coffee stain from this morning. I had a meeting and I spilled coffee on my shirt. 

Speaker 2: Sweetie, remember when we had breakfast? 

Speaker 4: Mommy, I don't want oatmeal! 

Speaker 2: Okay, how about we have a peanut butter and banana sandwich instead? 

Speaker 1: So, where's the boots? 

Speaker 5: No, I wanna have oatmeal! 

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, we can have oatmeal. 

Speaker 3: Can I have a sandwich too? 

Speaker 2: We can have sandwiches for all of us. 

Speaker 1: Alright, let's find those boots. 

Speaker 4: Mommy, I love you! 

Speaker 2: I love you too, sweetie. 

Speaker 1: So... 

Speaker 5: No! I wanna play with blocks! 

Speaker 2: Okay, honey, you can play with blocks after we find the boots. 

Speaker 1: Alright, where's the boots?

A cream-colored armchair with a blue blanket draped over it sits near a white oval coffee table with a book and a water bottle. A cluttered coat rack above the armchair displays a variety of winter hats, scarves, and two snow boots, one of which is currently being held by a young boy.

Speaker 1: Okay, so I'm almost done paying the bills, just this one more.

Speaker 2: Sweetie, how about we have leftover Chinese for lunch today? I was thinking we could heat it up and-

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, that's so cute, sweetie. Can you show me your picture later?

Speaker 1: Yeah, okay, just five more minutes.

Speaker 2: Uh, how about we make some hot chocolate to go with lunch?

Speaker 1: Sounds good to me.

Speaker 2: So, okay, I made a schedule for the kids tomorrow, remember when we were talking about Jack's playdate?

Speaker 1: Yeah, I think I saw it on my phone.

Speaker 2: Okay, so it says here that Jack's playdate is at 3 pm at Sarah's house.

Kid 2: Mommy, I'm hungry!

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, sweetie, just a minute. I'm almost done.

Speaker 1: I'm almost done paying this bill.

Speaker 2: Can you remember to pick up more yogurt on your way home from work today? We're almost out.

Speaker 1: Uh, yeah, I think I can do that.

Speaker 2: Like, I was thinking we could also get some broccoli for dinner tonight?

Speaker 1: Sounds good to me, but I have to run a report on this project first.

Speaker 2: Okay, honey, just go ahead and do that. I'll get started on dinner.

Kid 1: Mom, what's for dinner?

Speaker 2: We're going to have broccoli and... uh... what's the other thing we're having?

Speaker 1: I think you said yogurt?

Speaker 2: No, no, the other thing. Oh right, chicken!

Kid 2: Chicken!

Speaker 2: And, sweetie, can you please help me put the dishes away after we eat?

Kid 2: I don't wanna do it!

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, honey, it's not that big of a deal. Just help me for a minute, okay?

Speaker 1: I think I'm done paying the bills now.

Speaker 2: Great, okay so I'm going to start making lunch and-

Kid 1: Mommy, look at the snow outside! It's so pretty!

Speaker 2: Oh, yeah, it's beautiful, sweetie. I love listening to it snow.

Speaker 1: I'm glad I decided to work from home today.

Speaker 2: Yeah, me too. It's nice and quiet in here except for the kids.

Speaker 1: Okay, so I paid all the bills online, and now I'm trying to organize this paperwork, but it's just piling up.

Speaker 2: Sweetie, how about we make dinner plans for tonight? Remember when we went to that new Chinese restaurant last week?

Speaker 1: Yeah, it was really good, but, um, I'm not sure if I can make it. I have a meeting at 6 pm and I still need to prep for it.

Speaker 2: Okay so, what about we order something in then? Do you have any preferences?

Speaker 3 (son, 7 years old): Dad, can I have ice cream for dinner? Pleeease?

Speaker 1: Um, not tonight, buddy, we're trying to have a healthy meal. But, like, we can have some fruit or something.

Speaker 4 (daughter, 4 years old): (interrupting) Mommy, can I have juice!

Speaker 2: Okay sweetie, but only in your cup, not on the table. And after we eat, we can watch a movie together.

Speaker 1: That sounds good, yeah. (muttering to himself) Just got to finish this paperwork...

Speaker 5 (son): (excitedly) And can we get the new Lego set I've been wanting?

Speaker 2: (laughing) Okay, let's see, we'll talk about it later, okay? Daddy's busy right now.

Speaker 1: (distracted) Yeah, just give me a minute... (to himself) Where did I put that tax form...

Speaker 6 (daughter): (interrupting) Mommy, I drew a picture!

Speaker 2: (excitedly) Oh, sweetie, let me see! (to Speaker 1) And I think I found the schedule for the kids' school play, let's take a look.

Speaker 1: (distracted) Yeah, that's great, just... (looks at schedule) okay, so it's next Saturday...

Speaker 2: Okay so, that means we need to make sure the kids have their costumes by then. And, sweetie, how's your favorite book coming along?

Speaker 7 (son): (excitedly) Mom, can we go to the park tomorrow? I want to play on the swings!

Speaker 2: (smiling) Okay sweetie, we'll see, let me check the weather forecast. (to Speaker 1) And I think I found the recipe for that new salad we want to try.

Speaker 1: (distracted) Great, yeah, just... (looks at recipe) okay, so it's got, um, quinoa and stuff...

Speaker 2: Okay so, let's make a grocery list, then. And, sweetie, remember to finish your homework tonight.

Speaker 3 (son): (excitedly) Yeah, yeah, I'll do it! Can I have a snack first?

Speaker 2: (laughing) Okay, let's see what we have in the kitchen. (to Speaker 1) I'm going to go make some tea, you want some?

Speaker 1: (distracted) Yeah, that sounds great, thanks...

Speaker 1: Okay, so tonight we're going to do the laundry and get the dishwasher loaded, right, um, honey? I've got to finish this project for work by tomorrow morning.

Speaker 2: That's right, sweetie, and let's make sure the kids do their part, okay so, can you, uh, take charge of folding the clothes for now?

Speaker 3: But why do we have to fold the clothes? Can't the washing machine do it?

Speaker 2: No, sweetie, because then we won't have clean clothes to wear tomorrow.

Speaker 1: That's right, actually, the drying machine will help, but we still have to fold them, um, to put them away.

Speaker 4: Mommy, look! I drew a picture of our family.

Speaker 2: Oh, that's beautiful, honey, let me see it for a second.

Speaker 1: Okay, I think I've got about 90% done here, so let's, uh, get a move on, shall we?

Speaker 4: I want to help too! But why do we have to do laundry?

Speaker 2: Because when we wear clothes, they get dirty and we have to wash them to make them clean again.

Speaker 4: Oh, I see! Like when I spill juice on my shirt.

Speaker 1: Exactly, sweetie, you're a genius!

Speaker 3: Actually, I can help with putting the dishes away.

Speaker 2: That's great, sweetie, can you go put the plates in the right place?

Speaker 5: Me too! Me too!

Speaker 1: Okay, let's get this done, um, we'll take turns putting the dishes away.

Speaker 4: Can I help with the laundry now?

Speaker 2: Not yet, sweetie, you have to finish drawing your picture first.

Speaker 3: I know that the dishwasher was invented to help people wash dishes.

Speaker 1: That's right, actually, it was a big innovation, um, in the 1950s.

Speaker 5: No! I want to play with my Legos!

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, you can play later, after we finish the laundry and dishes.

Speaker 4: Can we have ice cream to celebrate when we're done?

Speaker 1: We'll see, honey, how about we have a snack of fruit instead?

Speaker 3: But why do we have to do laundry and dishes every week?

Speaker 2: Because it's part of keeping our home clean and tidy, remember when we used to have a cleaner come in?

Speaker 1: That's right, sweetie, but now we do it together as a family.

Speaker 5: Look! A bird outside!
